g.title: upstream_downstream_decision_diagram
g.text: For a crash near one signal, can the crash be placed before or after the signal along travel direction, or should it stay unresolved under the current conservative prototype?

g.title: input_scope
g.text: and row-level local flow already exist

g.title: rule_signal
g.text: use signals whose nearest row already has
crash and signal must stay on the same route

g.title: rule_area
g.text: near-signal geometry on the same route,
clipped to the speed-informed approach length,
then buffered and connected by a small hub

g.title: attachment_ok
g.text: Close enough to the chosen row

g.title: rule_attachment
g.text: to the selected row and signal context

g.title: phase2_hdr
g.text: order on one directed row and classify

g.title: compare_ok
g.text: Can crash and signal be reliably

g.title: rule_line
g.text: use the row-level flow result from diagram 1
to order crash and signal on one row,
then compare which comes first in travel direction

g.title: position
g.text: Is the crash before or after the signal

g.title: out_meaning
g.text: upstream = before the signal in travel direction
downstream = after the signal in travel direction

g.title: out_review
g.text: the optional later filter is not the core output
